title: cryptogramma crispa subsp. raddeana
intro: Cryptogramma crispa subsp. raddeana, also known as the Rocky Mountain rock fern, is a subspecies of the cryptogramma crispa plant, a type of fern that is native to the mountainous regions of North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful for treating various health issues. The health benefits of this plant include reducing inflammation, soothing skin irritations, and supporting the immune system. The herbal preparations of Cryptogramma crispa subsp. raddeana typically involve making teas, infusions, or tinctures from the plant's leaves, which can be consumed internally or applied topically to the affected area.
